child.name: resourceType
child.description: This is a MessageDefinition resource

child.name: id
child.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

child.name: meta
child.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not alwa...
child.longDesc: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: versionId
child.description: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is create...
child.longDesc: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.

child.name: lastUpdated
child.description: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.

child.name: source
child.description: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can...
child.longDesc: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.

child.name: profile
child.description: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a ref...
child.longDesc: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [[[StructureDefinition.url]]].

child.name: security
child.description: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructur...
child.longDesc: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: tag
child.description: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applic...
child.longDesc: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: implicitRules
child.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing...
child.longDesc: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

child.name: language
child.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

child.name: text
child.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a...
child.longDesc: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a h...
child.longDesc: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

child.name: div
child.description: The actual narrative content, a stripped down version of XHTML.

child.name: url
child.description: The business identifier that is used to reference the MessageDefinition and *is* expected to be consistent from server to server...
child.longDesc: The business identifier that is used to reference the MessageDefinition and *is* expected to be consistent from server to server.

child.name: identifier
child.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this message definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a...
child.longDesc: A formal identifier that is used to identify this message definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: version
child.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the message definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, ...
child.longDesc: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the message definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the message definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

child.name: versionAlgorithmString
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

child.name: versionAlgorithmCoding
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: name
child.description: A natural language name identifying the message definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machin...
child.longDesc: A natural language name identifying the message definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

child.name: title
child.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the message definition.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of this message definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

child.name: experimental
child.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this message definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) an...
child.longDesc: A Boolean value to indicate that this message definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

child.name: date
child.description: The date (and optionally time) when the message definition was last significantly changed. The date must change when the busine...
child.longDesc: The date (and optionally time) when the message definition was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the message definition changes.

child.name: publisher
child.description: The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the message definition.

child.name: contact
child.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: description
child.description: A free text natural language description of the message definition from a consumer's perspective.

child.name: useContext
child.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general cate...
child.longDesc: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate message definition instances.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A code that identifies the type of context being specified by this usage context.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueCodeableConcept
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: valueQuantity
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueRange
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: low
child.description: The low limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: high
child.description: The high limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueReference
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: jurisdiction
child.description: A legal or geographic region in which the message definition is intended to be used.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: purpose
child.description: Explanation of why this message definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

child.name: copyright
child.description: A copyright statement relating to the message definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restricti...
child.longDesc: A copyright statement relating to the message definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the message definition.

child.name: copyrightLabel
child.description: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period,...
child.longDesc: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are resctricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

child.name: replaces
child.description: A MessageDefinition that is superseded by this definition.

child.name: base
child.description: The MessageDefinition that is the basis for the contents of this resource.

child.name: parent
child.description: Identifies a protocol or workflow that this MessageDefinition represents a step in.

child.name: eventCoding
child.description: Event code or link to the EventDefinition.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: eventUri
child.description: Event code or link to the EventDefinition.

child.name: category
child.description: The impact of the content of the message.

child.name: focus
child.description: Identifies the resource (or resources) that are being addressed by the event. For example, the Encounter for an admit message o...
child.longDesc: Identifies the resource (or resources) that are being addressed by the event. For example, the Encounter for an admit message or two Account records for a merge.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: The kind of resource that must be the focus for this message.

child.name: profile
child.description: A profile that reflects constraints for the focal resource (and potentially for related resources).

child.name: min
child.description: Identifies the minimum number of resources of this type that must be pointed to by a message in order for it to be valid against...
child.longDesc: Identifies the minimum number of resources of this type that must be pointed to by a message in order for it to be valid against this MessageDefinition.

child.name: max
child.description: Identifies the maximum number of resources of this type that must be pointed to by a message in order for it to be valid against...
child.longDesc: Identifies the maximum number of resources of this type that must be pointed to by a message in order for it to be valid against this MessageDefinition.

child.name: responseRequired
child.description: Declare at a message definition level whether a response is required or only upon error or success, or never.

child.name: allowedResponse
child.description: Indicates what types of messages may be sent as an application-level response to this message.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: message
child.description: A reference to the message definition that must be adhered to by this supported response.

child.name: situation
child.description: Provides a description of the circumstances in which this response should be used (as opposed to one of the alternative response...
child.longDesc: Provides a description of the circumstances in which this response should be used (as opposed to one of the alternative responses).

child.name: graph
child.description: Graph is Canonical reference to a GraphDefinition. If a URL is provided, it is the canonical reference to a GraphDefinition that...
child.longDesc: Graph is Canonical reference to a GraphDefinition. If a URL is provided, it is the canonical reference to a GraphDefinition that it controls what additional resources are to be added to the Bundle when building the message. The GraphDefinition can also specify profiles that apply to the various resources.
